info.title: PowerFlex Installer REST API
info.description: Describes the Installer/Gateway API for PowerFlex

get.summary: Get all PowerFlex Installer commands in the queue
get.description:
Returns a list of commands in the Installer queue and their current status.

Authorized user roles:

SuperUser, SystemAdmin, LifecycleAdmin, StorageAdmin, Monitor, ReplicationManager, SnapshotManager, Support

post.description:
Clears the PowerFlex installer commands in the queue for the current process.

Authorized user roles:

SuperUser, SystemAdmin, LifecycleAdmin, Support

Abort a PowerFlex Installer process, specific command, or phase.

Authorized user roles:

SuperUser, SystemAdmin, LifecycleAdmin, Support

parameters.name: phase
parameters.description: Specify a phase to abort that specific phase. If the parameter value is left blank, the entire process will be aborted.

parameters.name: commandID
parameters.description: Command ID

post.description:
Retry current phase of specific command.

Authorized user roles:

SuperUser, SystemAdmin, LifecycleAdmin, Support

parameters.name: commandID
parameters.description: Command ID

get.description:
Retrieve current PowerFlex Installer operation and phase details.

Authorized user roles:

SuperUser, SystemAdmin, LifecycleAdmin, StorageAdmin, Monitor, ReplicationManager, SnapshotManager, Support

post.summary: Move PowerFlex installer to next phase
post.description:
Move the PowerFlex installer to the next phase in process.

Authorized user roles:

SuperUser, SystemAdmin, LifecycleAdmin, Support

post.summary: Move PowerFlex installer to idle phase
post.description:
Move the PowerFlex installer to idle phase, which will mark the end of the process, (but not cleaning of the queues).

Authorized user roles:

SuperUser, SystemAdmin, LifecycleAdmin, Support

Parse the CSV installation file into a JSON Configuration object.

The parameters for this resource as added as part of the HTTP header of the request. The parameters include the complete filepath of the CSV file, and the username and password of the PowerFlex Gateway. See the example below:

#token variable contains the Keycloak token that was returned from /auth/login or from /api/gatewayLogin
curl -v -k -X POST -H "Content-Type:multipart/form-data" -H "Authorization: Bearer ${token}" -F "file=@/root/3.
x_CSV-linux-3_node_3.csv" https://10.42.137.96/im/types/Configuration/instances/actions/parseFromCSV

Authorized user roles:

SuperUser, SystemAdmin, LifecycleAdmin, Support

Begin the installation or system extenstion process using the PowerFlex Installer.

Authorized user roles:

SuperUser, SystemAdmin, LifecycleAdmin, Support

parameters.name: noUpload
parameters.description: Skip upload package phase. By default, the value is set to false.

parameters.name: noInstall
parameters.description: Skip install package phase. By default, the value is set to false.

parameters.name: noConfigure
parameters.description: Skip configure phase and repository files. By default, the value is set to false.

parameters.name: noLinuxDevValidation
parameters.description: By default, the value is set to false.

parameters.name: extend
parameters.description: extend an existing system. By default, the value is set to false.

parameters.name: globalZeroPadPolicy
parameters.description: By default, the value is set to true.

parameters.name: runAllPhases
parameters.description: Move automatically between phases till the operation ends. By default, the value is set to false.

parameters.name: forceDeviceTakeover
parameters.description: Clean devices before installing SDS (without it installation will fail when it run after uninstall, must not be used in production)

parameters.name: noSecurityBootstrap
parameters.description: Skip security bootstrap. By default, the value is set to true.

post.summary: Run scripts on servers hosting the MDM or SDS
post.description:
PowerFlex can be used to run user-provided scripts on servers hosting MDM or SDS components. This feature is supported on Linux-based nodes only.

**Note**: This feature can be used for any purpose external to the PowerFlex system, such as running a set of CLI commands, patching an operating system, and more. The feature allows the running of scripts in a safe manner, both from a security and a data integrity perspective. For more information, see the Dell PowerFlex 4.6.x Administration Guide.

Up to one of the URI path parameters can be used at a time.

If none of the required parameters are used, then executeOnAllSdss or executeOnAllMdms or both must equal TRUE. If executeOnAllSdss or executeOnAllMdms equals TRUE, none of the required parameters should be used.

**Note**: Before running the liaRunOsPatching command, ensure that you log in and get the system configuration.

Authorized user roles:

SuperUser, SystemAdmin, LifecycleAdmin, Support

parameters.name: executeOnAllSdss
parameters.description: Run on all SDSs (true or false)

parameters.name: executeOnAllMdms
parameters.description: Run on all MDMs (true or false)

parameters.name: pdIds
parameters.description: List of Protection Domain IDs

parameters.name: fsIds
parameters.description: List of Fault Set IDs

parameters.name: sdsIds
parameters.description: List of SDS IDs

parameters.name: mdmIds
parameters.description: List of MDM IDs

parameters.name: isRebootRequired
parameters.description: Indicates if each node requires reboot after running the patch script (values: true or false)

parameters.name: isVerificationScriptRequired
parameters.description: Indicates if verification script should run on each node (values: true or false)

parameters.name: isRunningInParallelOnPds
parameters.description: Indicates if the operation should run in a parallel way on nodes that belong to different PDs (values: true/false)

parameters.name: isStopProcessingOnScriptFailure
parameters.description: should entire operation stop in case of a script failure (values: true/false)

parameters.name: TimeoutInMsec
parameters.description: Timeout for running the 'patch script' in milliseconds

parameters.name: isUploadFileNeeded
parameters.description: Indicates if GW  should upload scripts to the nodes (values: true/false)

parameters.name: patchScriptFilePath
parameters.description: Either local folder name or an http/https URL of 'patch script'. This parameter is required if isUploadFileNeeded value is true.

parameters.name: verificationScriptFilePath
parameters.description: Either local folder name or an http/https URL of verification script. This parameter is required if isUploadFileNeeded value is true.

parameters.name: maintenanceModeType
parameters.description: Maintenance Mode type (values: IMM/PMM)

parameters.name: verificationScriptTimeoutSec
parameters.description: Verification script timeout in seconds

parameters.name: rebootTimeoutSec
parameters.description: Node reboot timeout in seconds

post.description:
Authorized user roles:
SuperUser, SystemAdmin, LifecycleAdmin, Support

get.description:
Returns information about the current running orchestration (for example, install and upgrade) or external operation that is run by a different service). In case no operation is running, returns "idle" as operation name

Authorized user role:

SuperUser, SystemAdmin, LifecycleAdmin, StorageAdmin, Monitor, ReplicationManager, SnapshotManager, Support

post.description:
Sets gateway orchestration to idle. This API should be called only after restarting all gateway pods. 
For example, if the pod that runs operation restarts before the operation is back to idle, set it back by calling this API. This API will not stop the operation execution, just set the value to idle. To avoid a state in which operation is still running but was set to idle requires to restart the pods before calling this API.


Authorized user roles:

SuperUser, SystemAdmin, LifecycleAdmin, StorageAdmin, Monitor, ReplicationManager, SnapshotManager, Support

post.summary: Set the status of an external operation
post.description:
Sets the status (running - true/false) of an external operation.

External operation is a job:

Run by a different service but should not run in parallel with the GW operations. For example, reboot workflow that is

Run by node_lcm_starter.

Authorized user roles:
SuperUser, SystemAdmin, LifecycleAdmin, StorageAdmin, Monitor, ReplicationManager, SnapshotManager, Support

Performs NDU (non-disruptive upgrade), runs all the phases automatically (eliminates the need to use /im/types/ProcessPhase/actions/moveToNextPhase)

Authorized user roles:

SuperUser, SystemAdmin

error-message.title: error-message
error-message.description: Message component in Error response (Power API spec)

severity.description:
The severity of the condition.
        * INFO - Information that may be of use in understanding the failure. It is not a problem to fix.
        * WARNING - A condition that isn't a failure, but may be unexpected or a contributing factor. It may be necessary to fix the condition to successfully retry the request.
        * ERROR - An actual failure condition through which the request could not continue.
        * CRITICAL - A failure with significant impact to the system. Normally failed commands roll back and are just ERROR, but may be used for exceptional cases.

arguments.description:
Ordered list of substitution args for the error message. Must match up with 
            the {0}, {1}, etc... actually in the message referenced by the message code 
            above, if any.

BadRequest.description: HTTP 400 Bad Request Response: Cannot process request
